mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate profit deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative when both series trend upward concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism reverses direction in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher wage expenditures, which boost corporate earnings expectations and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and industrial production confirms synchronized demand and supply expansion, reducing inventory risks and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence between GDPC1 and INDPRO neutralizes the signal, or 60-day returns turn negative when both series expand simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation during a monetary tightening cycle forces central banks to restrict liquidity, raising borrowing costs and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration produces positive forward returns during tightening phases, or the signal loses significance when inflation remains below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales exceed 350,000 million dollars, robust consumer spending directly supports corporate top-line growth, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of instances where RSAFS crosses the 350,000 threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction during economic contractions.
